lines: Past the low wall, in the cold, they have the next one bound upright to a post. Wrists lashed to a crossbar, a gag in its mouth, a roped collar to keep it from sagging. Not nailed. Not opened. Not yet.

The black-gold has only begun to climb its arms, and a single bud of bone is pushing through the skin above one eye. It is still mostly a person. That is the point: they keep it whole and breathing until the one upstairs is finished.
A cult tally is pinned to the post beside it: 'First on the altar. This one for after. Cross it when the saint runs thin. Do not start it early; hunger ruins the lesson.'
It hears you breathing and tries, very slowly, to shape a word it has heard the other one whisper upstairs. Whatever you choose down here is yours to carry up with you.

effects.log: You leave it bound in the dark. It is still trying to find the word as you climb away.
You cut the ropes and end it before the cult can raise it. The cold gets a little colder, and a little quieter.

lines: It slumps off the post the moment the ropes part, and stops trying to speak. One fewer saint for the road to Hallowfen.
Whether that was mercy or meddling, you will not know until you reach the place this chapel keeps pointing at.
You leave it bound and breathing, kept for after, behind the low wall.
The cult keeps a patient larder. When the first saint runs thin they will hammer this one up in its place, and someone will have to come back down here.
